_comment: Golden corpus for the classifier. Seeded from the legacy test/classify.test.js. Drives BOTH the TS unit tests (expected outputs) and the parity test (legacy classify.js must agree with @pipeline/classify on every case). Grow this file with every real-world misclassification — see plan §14.
_comment_lang: Cases may carry a `lang` tag (default 'en') for the eval's per-language breakdown. The 'tr' cases are ASSUMED-representative renderings of well-known Turkish ATS/LinkedIn templates, NOT captured user mail — replace/extend them with real misclassified emails as they appear.

_comment_confidence: TS-only (classifyStatus). Not read by the parity gate. `low` = confidence < LOW_CONFIDENCE (flag for review); `reason` must appear in the reasons array.

_comment_roleClean: TS-only (cleanRole in aggregate.ts). Not read by the parity gate. Polishes extractRole output; `company` (optional) lets a trailing employer be stripped safely.

_comment_enrich: TS-only enrichment extractors (extract.ts). Not read by the parity gate. Every field is value-or-null; null cases are as important as positives.

_comment_matching: Matching/grouping layer (Known Issue A): threads reduced via threadsToApplications + boardFromApplications. Distinct employers sharing an ATS platform domain must stay DISTINCT groups — never bundle under the platform. expectCompanies (optional) is the exact group-name set, case-insensitive.

matching.name: greenhouse: two employers with sender names stay two groups

_comment_imapThreading: Desktop IMAP thread construction (root imap.js mapParsedToThreads). Keying threads on sender-domain+subject bundles different companies' boilerplate ATS mail into ONE thread (Known Issue A, matching layer). Each case: raw mails in, expected thread count out.

imapThreading.name: same company, ack + follow-up with Re: prefix → one thread
